mission.title: Microcks: Fix Blank API/Services List in UI
mission.description: Resolves the issue where the Microcks UI shows a blank page when listing APIs and Services, even though imports succeed. This is typically caused by MongoDB connection issues, Keycloak SSL requirement warnings, or ingress path misconfiguration.

steps.title: Check Microcks pod logs for errors
steps.description:
Look for SSL warnings or MongoDB errors in the Microcks app logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -n microcks -l app=microcks --tail=100 | grep -i 'WARN\|ERROR\|SSL\|mongo'
```
The most common cause is a Keycloak SSL warning: `SSL is required to authenticate. Remote address X.X.X.X is secure: false`. This blocks API listing requests silently.

steps.title: Check MongoDB connectivity
steps.description:
Verify MongoDB is running and Microcks can connect to it:
```bash
# Check MongoDB pod
kubectl get pods -n microcks -l app=mongodb

# Check MongoDB logs for connection issues
kubectl logs -n microcks -l app=mongodb --tail=30

# Verify Microcks can reach MongoDB
kubectl exec -n microcks deployment/microcks -- env | grep MONGO
```
If MongoDB is using persistent storage and the PVC is unbound, the pod will be stuck in `Pending`.

steps.title: Fix Keycloak SSL requirement (most common cause)
steps.description:
If the logs show the SSL warning, disable the SSL requirement on the microcks realm:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/microcks-keycloak -n microcks 9090:8080 &
sleep 2

KC_TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:9090/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token \
  -d 'username=admin&password=admin&grant_type=password&client_id=admin-cli' | jq -r '.access_token')

curl -s -X PUT http://localhost:9090/admin/realms/microcks \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $KC_TOKEN" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"sslRequired": "NONE"}'
```
This allows internal HTTP requests between Microcks and Keycloak without TLS.

steps.title: Verify the API list loads
steps.description:
Restart Microcks and check the UI:
```bash
kubectl rollout restart deployment/microcks -n microcks
kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=microcks -n microcks --timeout=120s
```
Refresh the browser. The API/Services list should now display imported specs.

resolution.summary: The blank API list is resolved by fixing the Keycloak SSL requirement mismatch or MongoDB connectivity. The UI silently fails to load API data when backend authentication requests are rejected.
